findings.evidence: `TauCeti/Analysis/PDE/EnergyForm.lean:102` expands both diagonals as `A.toQuadraticForm' U.2 + ⟪b, U.2⟫ * U.1 + c * U.1 ^ 2`; `TauCeti/Analysis/PDE/UniformEllipticity.lean:199` proves `(coefficientSymmetricPart A).toQuadraticForm' ξ = A.toQuadraticForm' ξ`, so no zero-drift assumption is used.
findings.fix: Generalize to `energyIntegrand_coefficientSymmetricPart_self (A) (b : EuclideanSpace ℝ n) (c) (U) : energyIntegrand (coefficientSymmetricPart A) b c U U = energyIntegrand A b c U U`, and derive the zero-drift specialization only if needed.
findings.issue: `energyIntegrand_coefficientSymmetricPart_zero_drift_self` unnecessarily fixes the drift to `0`; replacing only the principal coefficient by its symmetric part leaves the diagonal energy unchanged for any drift `b`.

summary: One symmetric-part diagonal lemma is stated at an unnecessarily special zero-drift level. The rest of the zero-drift symmetry API sits at the natural level for this file.
